generations.text:
I am writing to my estranged daughter, dear Eleanor,

It is with a heavy heart that I pen this letter to you, for I have not spoken in five years, and the storm is coming, and I am not sure I shall survive it. I am writing with trembling hands, for the storm is upon us, and the winds howl and the waves howl, and the sea howl.

My Dearest Daughter,

I write this letter with trembling hands, for I have not penned a word to thee in five years. The storm, which is now upon us, is upon the horizon of the tempest, which I know not to survive.
